summary: Split PR #2630 touched only src/melee/vi/vi0502.c, improving un_8031E444_OnEnter in the VI subsystem. The diff replaces raw byte indexing of the entry argument with a ViCharaDesc pointer, introduces role-specific GObj locals for fog/light/camera/model objects, simplifies a GXColor assignment, and applies small codegen-sensitive expression/local changes inside the model loop. The decomp-dev bot reported one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/vi/vi0502 un_8031E444_OnEnter from 98.92% to 99.66% (+4 bytes).

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains edits to un_8031E444_OnEnter.

what_changed.text: un_8031E444_OnEnter now casts arg to ViCharaDesc* desc instead of treating it as u8* input.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8* input = arg removed; ViCharaDesc* desc added; desc = (ViCharaDesc*) arg

what_changed.text: Character and costume access changed from input[0], input[1], input[3] to desc->p1_char_index, desc->p1_costume_index, and desc->p2_costume_index.
Generic HSD_GObj* gobj reuse was split into fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj locals.
erase_colors_vi0502 assignment was simplified from a casted GXColor pointer store to direct struct assignment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: *(GXColor*) &erase_colors_vi0502 = fog->color removed; erase_colors_vi0502 = fog->color added

what_changed.text: The model loop gained a jobj alias named new_var, used in HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, and changed the third gm_8016895C argument from 0 to (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0.
Float literals in animation requests changed from 0.0f to 0.0F.

smart_moves.text: Replacing byte-indexed arg access with ViCharaDesc field access improves type expression and makes the p1/p2 costume and character fields explicit.
Using separate GObj locals for fog, light, camera, and model objects makes object roles visible without changing the sequence of setup calls.
The PR was isolated to the planned src/melee/vi path slice and passed git diff --check per the PR body.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Created from origin/master as an isolated path slice
pr_body: git diff --check origin/master...HEAD passed for this slice

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an entry callback argument is known to be a descriptor struct, replacing u8* indexing with typed descriptor fields can both clarify intent and improve matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8* input removed; ViCharaDesc* desc added
raw_diff: desc field accesses replace input indices
decomp-dev bot: un_8031E444_OnEnter improved from 98.92% to 99.66%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Role-specific locals can improve readability in setup-heavy functions that create multiple GObj types in sequence.

naming_conventions.text: Use role-based local names for distinct GObj lifetimes, e.g. fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj, when the role is directly shown by the setup calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GObj_Create results assigned to fog_gobj/light_gobj/camera_gobj/model_gobj and passed to matching fog/light/camera/model setup calls

naming_conventions.text: char_index was used for the descriptor's p1_char_index value before passing it to viGetCharAnimByIndex.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8 char_index; char_index = desc->p1_char_index; viGetCharAnimByIndex(char_index)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced an alias local new_var for jobj in the model loop, using the alias for selected calls while retaining jobj for others.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_JObj* new_var; new_var = jobj; HSD_GObjObject_80390A70(..., new_var); HSD_JObjReqAnimAll(new_var, 0.0F)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR slice provides no rationale for why this alias is necessary; treat it as a narrow matching tactic, not a naming/style precedent.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed a literal zero argument to a computed zero expression, (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0, inside the already-null-checked model loop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This appears codegen-sensitive from the diff, but the PR evidence does not explain or independently verify the tactic beyond the bot's overall function improvement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct assignment to erase_colors_vi0502 replaced a casted pointer store to GXColor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a cleanup supported by the diff, but no separate type or symbol ownership discussion is present in the PR slice.

review_feedback.text: Reported 1 improvement in an unmatched item: main/melee/vi/vi0502 un_8031E444_OnEnter, +4 bytes, 98.92% before to 99.66% after.
The body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative for the slice once it completes, and local diff check passed.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs replacing u8* arg/input indexing with ViCharaDesc or descriptor field access in src/melee/vi.
Search for codegen-sensitive computed-zero expressions like '(ptr != NULL) * 0' to determine whether they are isolated tactics or a recurring MWCC pattern.
Search for remaining generic gobj reuse in setup-heavy VI functions where role-specific locals would improve reviewability.
Look for later cleanup of the local name new_var in src/melee/vi/vi0502.c.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed descriptor fields can replace byte-indexed callback args
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2630, un_8031E444_OnEnter improved when void* arg was cast to ViCharaDesc* and input[0]/input[1]/input[3] became desc->p1_char_index, desc->p1_costume_index, and desc->p2_costume_index. This is a graph-safe example of typed-field access improving reviewability and matching in src/melee/vi.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ViCharaDesc* desc and desc field accesses
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot 98.92% to 99.66% for un_8031E444_OnEnter

accepted_candidate_records.title: Computed-zero and alias locals require narrow evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2630 contains a jobj alias local named new_var and changes gm_8016895C's third argument from 0 to (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0. Preserve this only as an observed narrow tactic from this PR, not as a general style recommendation.

rejection_notes: Do not promote new_var as an accepted naming convention; the name is non-semantic and the slice gives no explanation beyond its use in the diff.
Do not treat the full preliminary branch report as the per-slice validation result; the PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative for the slice.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or review acceptance details; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
